title: Am I seeing things
wikitext:
[]: Am I seeing things?             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Wed Nov 09, 2005 3:28 pm In the platinum discs, when Asuka moves in with Shinji and Misato. Shinji walks to his door and Asuka opens it and this is where its funny, Shinji jumps from surprise, waves his hands in the air and when he does this another set of hands just appears in each corner. it looks really bad, was this in the original dvds/vhs? anyone else notice things like that?

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Wed Nov 09, 2005 4:29 pm Although I only have the Perfect collection, (I'm waiting for the Platinum thinpack collection to be released before I buy), I think you may find your answer in this thread: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=795

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Nov 09, 2005 6:04 pm it looks to be deliberate. Another of Shinji's various "crazy" takes. It's definitely not a telecining issue though.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Nov 09, 2005 11:14 pm Another pair of arms and then hands . Seems deliberate to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Wed Nov 09, 2005 11:53 pm Upon seeing the screenshot, I must agree that it is deliberate. Maybe I should keep my big mouth shut next time, until I buy the Platinum Thinpack.

Article: Am I seeing things
[]: Am I seeing things?             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Wed Nov 09, 2005 3:28 pm In the platinum discs, when Asuka moves in with Shinji and Misato. Shinji walks to his door and Asuka opens it and this is where its funny, Shinji jumps from surprise, waves his hands in the air and when he does this another set of hands just appears in each corner. it looks really bad, was this in the original dvds/vhs? anyone else notice things like that?

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Wed Nov 09, 2005 4:29 pm Although I only have the Perfect collection, (I'm waiting for the Platinum thinpack collection to be released before I buy), I think you may find your answer in this thread: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=795

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Nov 09, 2005 6:04 pm it looks to be deliberate. Another of Shinji's various "crazy" takes. It's definitely not a telecining issue though.
